text.title: Secrets Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Secrets Management methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: .env / *.key / *.pem / id_rsa / credentials.json / service-account*.json MUST be in .gitignore and rejected by pre-commit. History scan (git log --all -p | grep) MUST find zero matches against secret patterns.

rationale.source: GitHub Advanced Security + OWASP SCRA guidance

rule.rationale: Once pushed, a secret is compromised — even after rebase. Pre-commit is the only reliable boundary.

rule.statement: Service units load secrets via `EnvironmentFile=/etc/<service>.env` — never `Environment=` with the secret value inline. The file is referenced once per service.
rule.rationale: Inline Environment= values leak via `systemctl show`; EnvironmentFile is readable only by root + the unit's User.

rule.statement: .env files and EnvironmentFile= paths have mode 0600 owned by the service's User. `find /etc -name '*.env' -perm /o+r` MUST return empty.
rule.rationale: World-readable env files defeat the whole point; other-readable allows local privilege escalation.

rule.statement: Every secret class (DB, API key, JWT) has a single named owner accountable for rotation. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Rotation calendars stall without a named human.

rule.statement: On a known leak, the secret is rotated within 24h across every consumer named in the SecretsPlan. Plan validity is verified post-rotation.
rule.rationale: Rotating in one place and forgetting consumers is the standard incident-postmortem finding.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-secrets-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
